mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and lower recession probability, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the easing is not driven by acute crisis conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average during falling-FEDFUNDS windows, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals worsening labor-market stress and higher recession risk, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the signal loses sign consistency in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly RSAFS growth implies resilient consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice near-term earnings upward.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth fails to improve 20-day forward returns relative to weak-growth months, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is persistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in INDPRO with stable prices indicates stronger real production and better operating leverage for firms, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO growth does not lead to above-median 60-day forward returns, or if the effect reverses sign in expansions over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates are deeply negative and financial conditions are loose, which supports equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation running above 3% annualized while FEDFUNDS stays below 2%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-rate condition does not outperform unconditional returns, or if triggered observations produce negative 20-day returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in PAYEMS growth and rise in UNRATE identifies labor-market deterioration that signals weakening aggregate demand, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp within 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor deterioration signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns or if its walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST after a prior strength phase signals housing demand rollover and weaker forward consumption, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drawdowns are not followed by below-baseline 20-day returns, or if the signal becomes positive over a rolling 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with still-elevated CPIAUCSL implies faster real-rate pressure and multiple compression, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction does not predict negative 5-day returns during tightening episodes, or if positive returns dominate in periods when both rates and inflation are elevated.
